name: textX (Domain-Specific Language Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Igor Dejanovic / textX team

performance.notes: Arpeggio PEG parser backend; fast for small DSLs

agent_readiness.notes: Build DSLs using grammar + automatic AST model classes. from textx import metamodel_from_str. grammar = '''Model: entities+=Entity; Entity: 'entity' name=ID '{' attrs+=Attribute '}'; Attribute: name=ID ':' type=ID;'''. mm = metamodel_from_str(grammar). model = mm.model_from_str('entity Person { name: str age: int }'). model.entities[0].name → 'Person'. Validators: mm.register_obj_processors({'Entity': validate_entity}). Export: textx_textmate_grammar(mm, 'lang.tmLanguage'). jinja2 templates for code gen. VS Code extension support. Model-to-model transformation.

gotchas: Grammar debugging can be tricky — PEG parser errors not always clear
Complex ambiguous grammars require careful ordering
Limited error recovery — stops at first parse error
best_when: Building internal DSLs for configuration, modeling, or code generation
Rapid prototyping of domain models with text representations
When you need auto-generated Python classes from grammar rules
Model-to-text code generation with Jinja2 templates
avoid_when: Full programming language parsing (use ANTLR4 or tree-sitter)
When grammar reuse from existing ANTLR/Yacc grammars is needed
High-performance parsing of large files (use PLY or SLY)
summary: textX builds DSLs from grammar + auto-generates Python model classes. metamodel_from_str(grammar).model_from_str(text) → model objects. PEG grammar syntax. Auto-instantiated Python classes. Validators via obj_processors. Jinja2 code gen. VS Code support. MIT by Igor Dejanovic. Best for rapid DSL prototyping and model-to-text generation. No left recursion. Stops at first error. For complex languages use ANTLR4.
